comments.text: Perhaps, the reference to be added for Lovasz is [Lo66]. [Lo66]: L. Lovász, On covering of graphs, in: Theory of Graphs, (P. Erdős and G. Katona, eds.), Proc. Coll. at Tihany, Hungary, 1966, Academic Press and Akadémiai Kiadó, 231-236. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)

For chordal graphs, better bounds are known, see [EOZ08]. In [BLPPPV21], they considered partitions in $K_2$s and $K_3$s (with edges getting a larger weight). (The site has been updated to address this comment.)

There is progress on a closely related question by Győri and Keszegh (Combinatorica, Vol 37, 2017 pg. 1113-1124). In the case where $k>n^2/4$ and the graph $G$ is $K_4$-free the problem is equivalent to finding the minimum number of edge disjoint triangles in a graph $G$ with $k$ edges. This is precisely what Győri and Keszegh solve (and this special case was enquired about by Erdős). This references was located using GPT 5 Pro.
